title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo’s injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas of the Red Sox will resume batting practice Monday after a rib injury with no long-term concerns.
Pitcher Johan Oviedo to meet his Tommy John surgeon due to a right elbow strain affecting his pitch velocity.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval near rehab outings after injuries; Sandoval to start for Double-A Portland on Sunday.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, 22, a cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon in support of Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed with a rare thyroid cancer at 19 and credits MGH doctors for her successful treatment and recovery.
She aims to honor her medical team and the Adolescent and Young Adult program by participating in the marathon.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Boston Medical Center's social workers.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 for BMC, acknowledging social workers as crucial yet under-resourced in the healthcare system.
Her fundraising efforts include early morning runs and events, emphasizing the importance of community support for the mission.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, remaining winless this NWSL season.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasized patience and control in attack, citing the team's rushed play in the final third.
Amanda Gutierres, despite involvement in buildup, struggled with passing and has yet to score for Legacy FC.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr. charged with embezzlement for allegedly stealing hundreds of thousands from clients and unauthorized practice of law.
Rappa misused $660,000 given to him by a client for a trust account, delaying transfer and spending funds.
Suspended since 2025, Rappa continued practicing law and improperly handled a real estate closing, retaining client money.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a fiery car crash near Bedford, NH, after his vehicle hit a toll plaza.
Mirman, 51, was pulled from the wreckage by a state trooper and members of Gov. Kelly Ayotte’s security detail.
Officials report no charges yet; the single-car crash remains under investigation by New Hampshire State Police.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, charged after attacking a NH State Trooper in Dover parking lot.
Trooper assaulted while in parked cruiser; Newcomb allegedly attempted to seize his firearm.
Newcomb faces felony and misdemeanor charges; both men treated for minor injuries at hospital.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, from Lynnfield, will run the Boston Marathon for Mass General in memory of Sharla Sieve.
Sharla Sieve, Mallett's second mother, passed away from Stage 4 metastatic colorectal cancer in December 2022.
Mallett completed his first Boston Marathon last year, overcoming a calf injury at mile 17 to finish the race.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives anticipate New England Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1.
A trade for A.J. Brown is expected to become feasible for the Eagles due to salary-cap considerations post-June 1.
Eagles reportedly seek a first-round draft pick and more for A.J. Brown, while Patriots' interest remains undisclosed.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is now available on Netflix.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' documentary is streaming on PBS.
